A wider palate supports a more lifted face. Even as adults, improvement is possible with consistency. Be gentle. Do not ...
A wider palate supports a more lifted face. Even as adults, improvement is possible with consistency. Be gentle. Do not force it. Lightly lift the upper jaw while exhaling. A long face is often a dropped midface. When the tongue is not lifting up, the maxilla can stretch downward over time. T...
A long face is often a dropped midface. When the tongue is not lifting up, the maxilla can stretch downward over time. That’s why we focus on: 1. Tongue up 2. One third back suction 3. Keep your neck straight and posture aligned when mewing. The ears, shoulders, and neck should form a straight line, not ...
Keep your neck straight and posture aligned when mewing. The ears, shoulders, and neck should form a straight line, not forward like this. Good body posture makes mewing easier. Don’t worry. If this is your resting face, you may have chin tension. Too much tension can pull the mouth corners down a...
Don’t worry. If this is your resting face, you may have chin tension. Too much tension can pull the mouth corners down and tighten the brows. Keep your tongue up, relax the chin, and soften the brow area. When doing skincare, gently massage tight spots to release tension. #KokoHayashi #KokoFaceYoga #FaceYoga #FacePosture #CorrectPosture

I don’t think you’re mewing correctly. Poor tongue posture while growing up can lead to a longer face and less defined jawline, also called facial collapse. When Nat Geo Explorer @dr.shireenrahimi set out to find freshwater eels in the rivers of Mo’orea, French Polynesia, she didn’t expect it to take two days of searching and wading through rain-swollen water to finally reach them. Considered sacred on the island, these eels are a signal of a healthy ecosystem where clean, fresh water flows from the mo...

How To Find Sea Animals Easy‼️😱 If you’ve ever flipped over a rock in an Oregon tidepool and seen a small green or purpl...
How To Find Sea Animals Easy‼️😱 If you’ve ever flipped over a rock in an Oregon tidepool and seen a small green or purple crab sprint sideways at full speed… that’s likely a common shore crab. Common shore crabs live in the rocky intertidal zone, which means they survive daily extremes. Twice a day they’re underwater with waves crashing over them. ...

You Need to Check This Out‼️😱 On the Oregon coast, there’s a full housing market happening inside the tidepools. Hermit ...
You Need to Check This Out‼️😱 On the Oregon coast, there’s a full housing market happening inside the tidepools. Hermit crabs don’t grow their own shells. Their back half is soft and totally unprotected, so they rely on empty snail shells for survival. As they grow, they have to upgrade. But the shell has to be the perfect size. Too small and they ...
